dialogue.utterance: Dispatch to Unit 3 responding for unconscious/fainting/near-fainting at --Address Redacted--, Apt #7.
Hi sir, my name is Kyle and I'm an EMT with the rescue squad. What made you call 911 today?
I'm 57. I woke up and my left side started giving out on me.
Can you tell me your name and the date today?
My name is Tim. I think today's the 5th of November.
Can you look directly at me?
(Looks directly at EMT with slight left-sided droop noted.)
Where are you now?
I'm in Richmond, probably. I'm not sure about the county.
Can you squeeze both of my hands for me?
Sir, your left hand feels weaker than the right. It seems harder for you to hold the cane.
Sir, you're awake, alert, and oriented to person, place, time, and situation (AAOx4).
Yeah, I know who I am, where I am, and why I'm here.
When did you first notice the weakness on your left side?

dialogue.micro_intent: S.A.M.P.L.E. - events leading up to the event
dialogue.utterance: I went to bed around 9:30 and slept fine. When I woke up around 11:30, I tried to get up and my left leg kept giving out.
You're breathing, and your voice sounds clear. We're good on the airway.

dialogue.micro_intent: check breathing - Rate and depth of respirations
dialogue.utterance: Respirations are 16, regular, and unlabored.

dialogue.micro_intent: check breathing - Oxygen saturation measured with pulse oximetry
dialogue.utterance: Oxygen is at 100% on room air.

dialogue.micro_intent: check circulation - Pulse rate and quality
dialogue.utterance: Pulse is 62 and strong throughout.
Blood pressure is 151 over 100.
Skin appears warm, dry, and pink. No cyanosis observed.

dialogue.micro_intent: head and neck exam - Pupillary reflex / Size of pupils
dialogue.utterance: Let me check your eyes now. I'll use a light-stare at me please.

dialogue.micro_intent: head and neck exam - Pupillary reflex / Size of pupils
dialogue.utterance: Pupils are open and reactive, though slightly asymmetrical on the left.

dialogue.micro_intent: extremities and back exam - Gross motor and sensory function
dialogue.utterance: Let me look at your arms. Can you hold both arms out for me?
Right arm is stronger and remains steady; the left one drifts down a bit.
Let me check your blood sugar. It'll just take a second.
Pulse is currently 62.
Respirations are still 16 per minute.
Blood pressure remains 151 over 100.
Spo2 is 100% at the moment.
We're now accessing a vein in your right arm for an IV.
IV line is successfully in place in the right arm.
Given the neurological signs, we're activating Protocol 3-5 for stroke.
Pulse has decreased to 54 now.
Breathing is still 16, no change.
Blood pressure is now 159 over 91.
Blood sugar is 122 mg/dL.
Spo2 has dropped slightly to 96%.
EKG is still reading as sinus rhythm and stable.
Tell us about any conditions you have.
I have coronary artery disease, hypertension, hyperlipidemia, COPD, and I had a bypass a while back.
We are taking you to VCU Health because it is the closest stroke facility.
We'll now move you gently to the stretcher. I'll secure you with all the proper straps.
Stretcher is locked and ready for transport.
Patient is safely secured on the stretcher and transport is beginning.
Transporting now directly to VCU.
Blood pressure is steady at 159 over 91.
Oxygen saturation has not improved-still 96%.

dialogue.micro_intent: S.A.M.P.L.E. - events leading up to the event
dialogue.utterance: How many times have you fallen since you woke up?

dialogue.micro_intent: S.A.M.P.L.E. - events leading up to the event
dialogue.utterance: It's been three times, on my knees and my butt.

dialogue.role: nose, mouth and throat exam; EMT
dialogue.utterance: Quick check in your mouth and nose for any issues.

dialogue.role: nose, mouth and throat exam; Partner
dialogue.utterance: Speech appears slightly slurred, but the cause is unclear. He doesn't have all his dentures in.
I'm placing a stethoscope on your chest now.
Lungs are clear on both sides, no wheezing or crackles heard.
EKG is still showing a regular rhythm.
We're now setting up the four-lead EKG for monitoring during transport.
Monitor is attached and functioning without issues.
EKG is securely in place and will monitor the patient throughout transport.
List the medications you currently take.
I take Lisinopril, Amlodipine, Coreg, Lipitor, and others like Xarelto and Flovent.
Any known allergies to medications or foods?
I'm only allergic to Bactrim.
On a scale from 0 to 10, how much pain are you experiencing?
Zero. I don't feel any pain.
Protocol remains stroke/tia (3-5). No pain reported.
Care is complete and transferred to ER CT2 staff.
